content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
July 8,
1994
Honorable Patricio Miranda
City of Irwindale
5050 North Irwindale Avenue
Irwindale,
CA
91706
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No.
1—94—216
Dear Councilmember Miranda:
This is in response to your letter requesting advice
concerning your duties under the conflict—of—interest provisions
of the Political Reform Act
(the “Act”)
1 as a member of the
Irwindale Redewelopment Agency Board of Directors.
Please note that formal written advice is the application of
the law to a specific set of facts.
Since your request does not
provide the facts of a specific decision,
we are treating your
request as one for informal assistance.
2
The Commission does not
act as the finder of fact in providing advice.
(In re Oglesby
(1975)
1 FPPC Ops.
71.)
OUESTION
May you participate in decisions of the Irwindale Community
Redevelopment Agency Board of Directors to develop guidelines for
participation in the purchase of homes in the Manning Housing II
Single Family Housing Project within the City of Irwindale?
1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations,
Sections
18000—18954.
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
2
Please note,
informal assistanc does not provide the requestor
with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c) (3).)
428 J Street, Suite 800 •
P.O. Box 807 •
Sacramento, CA
95804-0807 •
(916) 322-5660


Our File No.
1-94—216
Page
2
CONCLUSION
You may participate in the decisions so long as the decisions
concerning the proposed guidelines will not have a reasonably
foreseeable material financial effect on your real property
interests or sources of income.
FACTS
3
Several years ago, the Irwindale Community Redevelopment
Agency (the “Agency”)
purchased property for the development of
thirty—six housing units for sale to
Low— and Moderate—Income
residents.
The Agency will shortly be reviewing development
guidelines for participation in the purchase of homes in the
project, the Manning Housing II Single Family Housing Project.
The guidelines will deal with a variety of issues,
including
defining the eligibility requirements for purchasing a project
unit (residence requirements and eligibility priorities)
and
application procedures.
You are a councilinember of the City of Irwindale and a member
of the Agency Board of Directors.
The City of Irwindale has
approximately 1,050 residents and less than 300 residential units.
You own commercial property in the City of Irwindale which is
approximately 465 feet from the Agency-owned development site.
The commercial property is leased and operated as a tire business.
ANALYSIS
Economic Interests
Section 87100 of the Act prohibits any public official from
making, participating in making,
or otherwise using his or her
official position to influence a governmental decision in which
the official has a financial interest.
As a member of the
Irwindale City Council and as a member of the Agency Board of
Directors,
you are a public official undei the Act.
(Section
82048.)
-
Section 87103 specifies that a public official has a
financial interest in a decision if it is reasonably foreseeable
that the decision will have a material financial effect,
distinguishable from the effect on the public generally,
on the
official or a member of his or her immediate family or on any of
the official’s economic interests.
This would include real
property in which the public official has a direct or indirect
interest worth $1000 or more,
or any source of income aggregating
$250 or more in value provided to, received by or promised to the
Facts provided for the Miranda Advice Letter,
No.
A-94—014,
have been incorporated.


Our File No.
1-94-216
Page
3
public official within 12 months prior to the time when the
decision is made.
(Section 87103(b)
and (c).)
We previously advised you that you may participate in Agency
decisions so long as the decisions will not have a reasonably
foreseeable
4 material financial effect on your commercial
property,
which is less than 2,500 feet from the project site,
or
your sources of income, the business tenants.
(Sections 87103(b)
and (c); Miranda Advice Letter, supra. )
We also advised you that if governmental decisions,
such as
the development of construction guidelines and the selection of a
developer for construction of the project area, are ones which
merely implement major policy decisions,
you may participate in
the decisions if they will not independently materially affect
your economic interests.
(Miranda Advice Letter, supra; Athan
Advice Letter,
No.
A—86-094.)
It appears that the development of the guidelines for
purchase of project homes will not affect the existence of the
project as a whole.
Thus, these decisions are similar
implementation decisions as those discussed in our prior advice
letter.
Therefore,
you may participate in the decisions so long
as they independently will not have a reasonably foreseeable
material financial effect on your real property or your sources of
income as set forth in Regulations 18702.2 and 18702.3.
The sample guidelines you provided indicate thatthe
decisions will pertain to the establishment of eligibility
requirements for purchasing a project unit
(residence requirements
and eligibility priorities)
and application procedures.
On the
basis of these facts, it appears unlikely that such guideline
decisions would have a material financial effect on your property
or business tenants.
However,
as the guidelines are formulated
An effect is considered reasonably foreseeable if there is a
substantial likelihood that it will occur.,
Certainty is not
required.
However, if an effect is only a mere possibility, it is
not reasonably foreseeable.
(In re Thorner
(1975)
1 FPPC Ops.
198.)
In our prior advice letter,
we also discussed the materiality
standards which may apply to your economic interests and the
“public generally” exception.
(Regulations 18702.1
— 18702.6;
Regulation 18703.)
It appears that the decisions concerning the
guidelines for purchase of project homes would affect your
interests indirectly.
Thus, the relevant standard to determine
whether there is a reasonably foreseeable material financial
effect on your real property interest is Regulation 18702.3 and to
determine materiality with respect to your sources of income,
which is a business entity, is Regulation 18702.2.
You have
provide no facts to indicate that the “public generally” exception
would apply.


Our File No.
1—94—216
Page
4
you should examine each decision independently to determine if
there are specific facts which require a contrary conclusion.
If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at
(916)
322_5901.6
Sincerely,
Steven G.
Churchwell
General Counsel
By:
Luisa Menchaca
Counsel,
Legal Division
6
Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries.
Alternatively,
copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of
1Ocz per page.

Single Family Housing Project within the City of Irwindale?
; Regulation 18329(c) (3).)
 •
(916) 322-5660


Our File No.
1-94—216

concerning the proposed guidelines will not have a reasonably
foreseeable material financial effect on your real property
interests or sources of income.
